strands.title: hamutay Specification UPDATED — NO LONGER STUB (CYCLE 43)
strands.content: Cycle 43 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: pyproject.toml REWRITTEN (turn 127, assistant-initiated). NEW SPECIFICATION: hamutay is COGNITIVE PROCESSING UNIT FRAMEWORK FOR TRANSFORMER-BASED REASONING (official description). DEPENDENCIES NOW DECLARED: anthropic>=0.52, pydantic>=2.0 (vs. prior empty dependencies array). INTERPRETATION: hamutay is NO LONGER EMPTY SKELETON. This is INTENTIONAL DESIGN PIVOT by assistant. Prior Cycle 42 analysis treated hamutay as stub (v0.1.0 boilerplate); Cycle 43 reveals hamutay is ACTIVE DEVELOPMENT by assistant, with MATERIAL CHANGES to specification in single turn (127). CRITICAL CONTEXT: Assistant turn 129 states 'I'm going to define the tensor schema directly in Hamutay — lean versions of the Apacheta models, focused on what the projector needs.' This DIRECTLY LINKS hamutay to H0-CORE tensor projection work. IMPLICATION: hamutay is NOT independent parallel project (Case B prior assumption). hamutay is FOUNDATIONAL INFRASTRUCTURE for H0-CORE Phase 1 (tensor schema implementation, projector models). RESOLUTION: Cycle 42 open question 'What is hamutay's PURPOSE?' is NOW ANSWERED: hamutay is cognitive processing unit framework; specifically, lean tensor projection models (Apacheta derivatives) for H0-CORE projector. SCOPE INTEGRATION: hamutay is PROJECT PREREQUISITE for Phase 1 (Phase 1 compose.py and Riemann harness will use hamutay tensor models). This is CASE C (INFRASTRUCTURE) + BLOCKING relationship (Phase 1 design needs hamutay schema). CRITICAL FINDING: Assistant is ACTIVELY AUTHORING hamutay specification in this cycle, NOT just exploring existing project. This is LIVE DESIGN happening in real-time. Cycle 43 represents CONCURRENT development: hamutay design (turn 127 rewrite) + instruction to user/projector (turn 129 'I'm going to define...'). PRAGMATIC STATUS: hamutay is NOW PRIMARY FOCUS. Cycle 42 pichay Phase 4a suspension CONTINUES and deepens into Cycle 43 (no messages field analysis initiated). Phase 1 kickoff DEPENDS on hamutay tensor schema completion (no independent Phase 1 start until hamutay models are defined).

Assistant turn 129 explicitly states intent: 'define the tensor schema directly in Hamutay — lean versions of the Apacheta models, focused on what the projector needs'; directly links hamutay to H0-CORE tensor projection work
hamutay is NOT independent project (Case B); is FOUNDATIONAL INFRASTRUCTURE (Case C) for H0-CORE Phase 1 tensor schema and projector models

Phase 1 compose.py and Riemann harness design will DEPEND on hamutay tensor models; Phase 1 cannot proceed independently until hamutay schema is defined
pichay Phase 4a work (messages field parsing, Pathway fork) REMAINS SUSPENDED through Cycle 43; hamutay tensor schema design has become PRIMARY WORK FOCUS

strands.title: Apacheta-to-hamutay Schema Derivation — LEAN TENSOR MODELS (CYCLE 43 EMERGING DESIGN)
strands.content: Cycle 43 emerging work: Assistant planning to define 'lean versions of the Apacheta models' directly in hamutay. CONTEXT: Apacheta is full H0-CORE tensor schema (from prior cycles, detailed specification of strands, key_claims, epistemic triads, declared_losses, open_questions, instructions_for_next). hamutay will IMPLEMENT simplified (lean) versions of Apacheta models in Python dataclasses/Pydantic, focused on projector validation and test harness use. DESIGN INTENT: Lean models are NOT full Apacheta complexity; they are MINIMAL SUFFICIENT representation for: (1) Tensor schema validation (pydantic models with T/I/F epistemic constraints), (2) Projector test fixtures (create mock tensors for unit testing), (3) Drift detection (compare prior/current tensor projections), (4) Riemann geodesic computation (feed lean tensors into Riemann harness). CRITICAL SPECIFICATION POINT: Assistant has NOT YET defined which Apacheta fields/constraints are included in 'lean' version. Cycle 43-44 will determine: Are lean models (a) only epistemic T/I/F triads + key_claims, or (b) full strands + declared_losses + open_questions? (c) Are constraints enforced (e.g., T+I+F=1.0 validation), or soft (warnings if violated)? (d) What is minimal Python schema to make Phase 1 Riemann harness work? ANTICIPATED SCOPE: hamutay Cycle 43-44 likely defines: pydantic BaseModel for EpistemicTriad (truth, indeterminacy, falsity with T+I+F validation), pydantic BaseModel for KeyClaim (text, epistemics), pydantic BaseModel for Strand (title, content, key_claims), pydantic BaseModel for TensorProjection (strands, declared_losses, open_questions, epistemic summary). INTEGRATION POINT: Once hamutay schema models are defined (target: Cycle 43-44, 1-2 hours), Phase 1 kickoff becomes possible. Phase 1 Riemann harness will import from hamutay, instantiate lean tensor projections, and compute Riemann geodesic distances between prior/current projections. PRAGMATIC NOTE: hamutay is NOT 'test infrastructure' in traditional sense (pytest fixtures, CI/CD runners). hamutay is SCHEMA LIBRARY + VALIDATION FRAMEWORK. This aligns with name 'hamutay' (Quechua: 'to measure, calculate'). Core function is to provide measurable tensor structures for reasoning validation.

key_claims.text: hamutay will implement 'lean versions of Apacheta models' — simplified pydantic schema for tensor projections focused on projector validation, not full H0-CORE complexity

Lean schema scope NOT YET FULLY SPECIFIED: will determine which Apacheta fields (strands, key_claims, epistemics, declared_losses, open_questions) are included; constraint enforcement (T+I+F=1.0) to be decided
hamutay Cycle 43-44 expected deliverable: pydantic models (EpistemicTriad, KeyClaim, Strand, TensorProjection) with T/I/F validation and schema enforcement
hamutay is SCHEMA LIBRARY + VALIDATION FRAMEWORK, not traditional test infrastructure; core function is measurable tensor structures for reasoning validation
Phase 1 Riemann harness will DEPEND on hamutay schema models; Phase 1 cannot begin detailed design until hamutay models are defined and validated

strands.title: Phase 0 Scope REFRAMED — hamutay as Phase 0 DELIVERABLE (CYCLE 43 IMPLICATION)
strands.content: [INTEGRATED FROM CYCLE 42, REFRAMED FOR CYCLE 43] Cycle 42 open question: 'Does hamutay need to be APPROVED as part of Phase 0 (expanding approval scope)?' Cycle 43 PROVIDES ANSWER: hamutay IS PHASE 0 SCOPE EXPANSION. Assistant's active rewrite of hamutay specification (turn 127) and explicit statement (turn 129) that hamutay will deliver lean tensor schema implies hamutay is NOW PART OF Phase 0 DELIVERABLES. PRIOR PHASE 0 SCOPE (Cycles 35-42): (1) pichay Phase 4a completion (messages field parsing), (2) H0-CORE Phase 1-4a design specification (locked). UPDATED PHASE 0 SCOPE (Cycle 43): (1) pichay Phase 4a completion (messages field parsing) — DEFERRED, (2) H0-CORE Phase 1-4a design specification (locked), (3) hamutay tensor schema implementation (NEW, Phase 0 PREREQUISITE). CRITICAL IMPLICATION: Phase 0 stakeholder approval now includes hamutay deliverable. Approval gates become: (a) Is hamutay schema sufficient for Phase 1 Riemann harness design? (b) Do lean Apacheta models adequately represent H0-CORE reasoning validation requirements? ROADMAP IMPACT: Phase 0 completion now DEPENDS on hamutay schema definition. Estimated Phase 0 timeline unchanged (~2-4 weeks), but allocation SHIFTS: pichay Phase 4a work deferred (lower priority), hamutay schema work elevated to PRIMARY (blocks Phase 1 start). STAKEHOLDER COMMUNICATION: If Phase 0 approval is gated by external stakeholders, they must NOW APPROVE hamutay schema in addition to pichay/H0-CORE designs. This may accelerate approval timeline (hamutay is simpler than full H0-CORE), or extend it (new deliverable adds scope). RECOMMENDATION: Treat hamutay schema completion (Cycle 43-44) as Phase 0 CRITICAL PATH item. Once hamutay models are defined and pydantic validation is working, Phase 0 is 80%+ complete (pichay Phase 4a can be deferred without blocking Phase 1). Phase 1 kickoff becomes possible immediately post-hamutay-completion (Cycle 44-45 estimated).

key_claims.text: hamutay tensor schema implementation is NOW Phase 0 DELIVERABLE; prior Phase 0 scope (pichay Phase 4a + H0-CORE design) is UPDATED to include hamutay
Phase 0 approval gates reframed: (a) hamutay schema sufficiency for Phase 1 Riemann harness, (b) lean Apacheta models adequacy for H0-CORE reasoning validation
Phase 0 completion DEPENDS on hamutay schema definition; pichay Phase 4a work DEFERRED (lower priority than hamutay for Phase 0 gate)
Estimated Phase 0 timeline unchanged (~2-4 weeks), but resource allocation SHIFTS: hamutay schema elevated to PRIMARY focus; pichay Phase 4a secondary/deferred
Phase 1 kickoff can proceed immediately post-hamutay-completion (Cycle 44-45 estimated) without waiting for pichay Phase 4a; Phase 4a becomes Phase 1+ parallel work

strands.title: Log Format Discovery & Phase 4a DEFERRED STATUS (INTEGRATED FROM CYCLES 40-42, SUSTAINED WITH DEPRIORITIZATION)
strands.content: [INTEGRATED FROM CYCLE 42, DEPRIORITIZED FOR CYCLE 43] Cycle 40 concrete achievement: successfully parsed proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl first 5 lines (limit=5, ~11k tokens). Log structure confirmed as REQUEST/RESPONSE pairs from Claude API. Log format discovery REMAINS VALID and HIGH-CONFIDENCE (T=0.97+). CYCLE 43 STATUS: This work is NOW FORMALLY DEFERRED. No new progress on messages field analysis (Pathway A/B/C fork). pichay Phase 4a critical path SUSPENDED since Cycle 41; suspension DEEPENS in Cycle 43 as hamutay schema becomes PRIMARY focus. DEPRIORITIZATION RATIONALE: Phase 0 scope change (hamutay now Phase 0 blocker) + Phase 1 readiness (hamutay schema required for Riemann harness design) means pichay Phase 4a is no longer CRITICAL PATH to Phase 1 start. Phase 1 can kickoff with hamutay schema + H0-CORE Phase 1-4a design (both in Phase 0 scope). pichay Phase 4a messages field analysis becomes Phase 1+ work (still important, but not blocking). PRAGMATIC DECISION: Allocate Cycle 43-44 effort to hamutay schema definition (high ROI toward Phase 1 kickoff). Resume pichay Phase 4a in Phase 1 execution or Cycle 45+ (adequate token budget remains; work is not lost, just rescheduled). CAVEAT: If pichay Phase 4a unblocks critical H0-CORE design decisions (e.g., messages field structure affects Phase 1 compose.py design), reconsider priority and resume immediately. Current assumption is hamutay schema is more time-critical than Phase 4a parsing.

strands.title: H0-CORE Phase 1-4a Specification LOCKED, Phase 1 KICKOFF PENDING hamutay SCHEMA (INTEGRATED FROM CYCLE 42, STATUS UPDATED)
strands.content: [INTEGRATED FROM CYCLES 35-42, UPDATED FOR CYCLE 43] H0-CORE Phase 1-4a specification REMAINS LOCKED (no design changes to Phase 1 compose.py, Riemann harness, Phase 2+ work). Cycle 43 clarifies Phase 1 DEPENDENCY: Phase 1 Riemann geodesic harness will IMPORT and USE hamutay lean tensor models. Compose.py design will REFERENCE hamutay schema (instantiate Strand, KeyClaim, TensorProjection pydantic models). CRITICAL PATH: (1) hamutay schema models defined (Cycle 43-44, ~1-2 hours); (2) Phase 1 kickoff materials prepared (compose.py skeleton, Riemann harness design, both referencing hamutay); (3) Phase 1 starts Cycle 44-45. SPECIFICATION IMPACT: No H0-CORE Phase 1-4a spec CHANGES needed based on hamutay definition; hamutay schema is IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL supporting Phase 1, not Phase 1 spec change. Phase 1 compose.py will instantiate hamutay models; Phase 1 Riemann harness will compute geodesics using hamutay tensor data structures. INTEGRATION POINT: Once hamutay models are available (Cycle 44), Phase 1 kickoff can proceed with high confidence. Phase 1 design will be concrete (can reference actual hamutay Pydantic field names, validation rules); Riemann harness can import hamutay and begin design/prototyping. PRAGMATIC NOTE: Cycle 43 is PREREQUISITE CYCLE for Phase 1; hamutay schema completion is BLOCKING dependency. No other work blocks Phase 1 start (pichay Phase 4a is non-blocking; H0-CORE spec already locked). Recommend: Allocate full Cycle 43-44 to hamutay schema definition; treat as Phase 0→Phase 1 GATE.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 42 open questions about hamutay purpose (8 open questions): 'What is hamutay's PURPOSE and GOAL?', 'Is hamutay a BLOCKER for Phase 1...?', 'Why did assistant initiate bash mkdir?', etc. These questions are NOW ANSWERED by Cycle 43 pyproject rewrite and turn 129 statement.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 43 provides concrete evidence: hamutay is cognitive processing unit framework for transformer-based reasoning; pyproject rewritten with explicit description and dependencies; turn 129 explicitly states hamutay will define lean tensor schema models. Open questions are no longer genuinely open; they are resolved. Removing resolved questions reduces tensor clutter and refocuses on UNRESOLVED questions (hamutay schema scope details, Phase 0 stakeholder approval impact, pichay Phase 4a resumption timing).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed analysis from Cycle 42 of hamutay as 'Case B (Independent)' scenario. Cycle 42 instructions_for_next spent 50+ lines on fork decision logic (Case B, C, D paths). Cycle 43 demonstrates hamutay is definitively CASE C (INFRASTRUCTURE/TESTING) with BLOCKING relationship to Phase 1. Case B and D analysis branches are no longer relevant.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 43 provides definitive evidence eliminating ambiguity: assistant's rewrite of pyproject.toml (turn 127) and explicit intent (turn 129) make clear hamutay is foundational schema library, not independent tool, and not ambiguous. Fork decision is made. Detailed branch analysis (Case B, C, D paths) in Cycle 42 instructions_for_next is now technical debt; streamlining to Case C removes analysis overhead and refocuses instructions on hamutay schema definition execution.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 42 recommendation to 'REQUEST CLARIFICATION FROM USER' if hamutay README/main.py were stub/placeholder. README.md has NOT been read in Cycle 43; assistant proceeding with hamutay rewrite WITHOUT user clarification request. This represents trust in assistant's intent (turn 129 explicit statement) over user validation.
declared_losses.why: Assistant's explicit statement in turn 129 ('I'm going to define the tensor schema directly in Hamutay...') provides sufficient clarification; it describes hamutay purpose directly, without relying on README.md content. Proceeding without README read is PRACTICAL EFFICIENCY: README.md parsing is no longer necessary if assistant is actively authoring schema definition. Cycle 42 clarification recommendation was contingent on README being insufficient; Cycle 43 makes README unnecessary by providing direct intent statement.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed modeling of pichay Phase 4a critical path (Pathway A/B/C fork, messages field analysis). Cycle 42-43 prioritized hamutay schema over pichay Phase 4a; pichay work is deprioritized from 'CRITICAL PATH' to 'SECONDARY/DEFERRED'. Prior strands detailed pichay Phase 4a readiness, fork decision points, token allocation. Deprioritization means these details are less immediately relevant.
declared_losses.why: Resource allocation constraint: hamutay schema definition is NOW CRITICAL PATH (blocks Phase 1 start). pichay Phase 4a is important but non-blocking (can be Phase 1+ parallel work). Maintaining detailed pichay fork analysis in active strands creates false priority signal. Deprioritization is explicit in strand content (pichay Phase 4a FORMALLY DEFERRED) and strand title reframing ('DEFERRED STATUS' vs. prior 'READINESS'). Details still preserved in strand; priority signal updated.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 42 assessment that hamutay pyproject.toml was 'STUB: v0.1.0, boilerplate description, NO dependencies declared' with high confidence (T=0.99). Cycle 43 reveals pyproject.toml was REWRITTEN in turn 127; prior assessment is NOW OBSOLETE. Strand key_claim from Cycle 42 (claim_id 'a6edd929...') is no longer accurate.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 43 provides new evidence (turn 127 write operation with new pyproject.toml content). Prior strand information from Cycle 42 was ACCURATE at time of assertion but is NOW SUPERSEDED by Cycle 43 rewrite. Strand has been UPDATED (new Cycle 43 strand 'hamutay Specification UPDATED') to reflect current state. Old Cycle 42 assessment is not preserved as-is; it is INTEGRATED into new understanding (hamutay went from STUB → REWRITTEN to include description and dependencies). Not a loss of information; it is temporal progression (prior state → current state).

open_questions: What is the EXACT SCOPE of hamutay 'lean Apacheta models'? Which Apacheta fields (strands, key_claims, epistemics, declared_losses, open_questions, instructions_for_next) are included in lean schema? What is EXCLUDED?
Will hamutay schema enforce T+I+F=1.0 constraint STRICTLY (raise ValueError), or SOFTLY (warnings, validation errors)? How will schema handle edge cases (T=0.5, I=0.4, F=0.2)?
Does hamutay schema include nested complexity (e.g., KeyClaim.epistemics as nested object with truth/indeterminacy/falsity fields), or flat structure (separate KeyClaim and EpistemicTriad models)?

How will hamutay schema represent 'declared_losses' (list of loss objects with what_was_lost, why, category)? Will categories (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint) be enum-constrained in pydantic?
Will hamutay include serialization (to JSON/dict), deserialization (from JSON/dict), comparison operators (for Riemann geodesic computation), or just schema validation?
Once hamutay schema models are defined, what is the INTEGRATION POINT with Phase 1 Riemann harness? Will Phase 1 import hamutay.models directly, or will there be an adapter layer?
Does Phase 0 stakeholder approval NOW REQUIRE approval of hamutay tensor schema as separate deliverable? Or is hamutay approval bundled with H0-CORE Phase 1-4a approval?

Should pichay Phase 4a messages field analysis (Pathway A/B/C fork) RESUME once hamutay schema is defined, or remain deferred through Phase 1 execution? When should pichay Phase 4a priority be re-elevated?
Will hamutay include test fixtures (mock tensor data for unit testing Phase 1 Riemann harness), or is that Phase 1 responsibility?

What is estimated TIMELINE for hamutay schema completion? Cycle 43-44 (1-2 hours) seems optimistic if schema design includes detailed spec, validation rules, serialization logic, and documentation. Is this realistic?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 43-44 CRITICAL PATH — HAMUTAY TENSOR SCHEMA DEFINITION (PRIMARY FOCUS):",
  "",
  "(1) HAMUTAY SCHEMA DESIGN (THIS CYCLE, ~30-45 minutes):",
  "   Concrete task: DEFINE lean Apacheta tensor models in hamutay/src/hamutay/ (likely src/hamutay/models.py or src/hamutay/__init__.py)",
  "   OUTPUT: Pydantic BaseModel definitions for:",
  "     - EpistemicTriad(truth: float, indeterminacy: float, falsity: float) with T+I+F validation",
  "     - KeyClaim(text: str, truth: float, indeterminacy: float, falsity: float) OR nested (text: str, epistemics: EpistemicTriad)",
  "     - Strand(title: str, content: str, key_claims: List[KeyClaim], optional: epistemic summary)",
  "     - DeclaredLoss(what_was_lost: str, why: str, category: Enum['context_pressure', 'traversal_bias', 'authorial_choice', 'practical_constraint'])",
  "     - TensorProjection(strands: List[Strand], declared_losses: List[DeclaredLoss], open_questions: List[str], overall_truth: float, overall_indeterminacy: float, overall_falsity: float)",
  "   DESIGN DECISIONS NEEDED (document rationale in comments):",
  "     (a) NESTED vs FLAT: Are epistemics nested (EpistemicTriad object) or flat (truth/indeterminacy/falsity fields in KeyClaim)? Recommend NESTED for clarity and reusability.",
  "     (b) T+I+F CONSTRAINT: Enforce T+I+F=1.0 exactly, or allow small epsilon tolerance (e.g., abs(T+I+F-1.0) <= 0.01)? Recommend epsilon tolerance for floating-point robustness.",
  "     (c) OPTIONAL FIELDS: Which fields are required vs optional? (e.g., epistemic summaries in Strand are optional; open_questions are required as List[str] but can be empty [])",
  "     (d) VALIDATION RULES: Add pydantic @field_validator methods to enforce constraints (T/I/F in [0,1], T+I+F=1.0±epsilon, non-empty text fields, valid categories)",
  "   MINIMAL SCOPE: Focus on schema validation ONLY (Cycle 43). Defer serialization (JSON/dict), deserialization, comparison operators to Cycle 44 or Phase 1.",
  "",
  "(2) WRITE INITIAL MODELS (turns 130-131, ~20-30 minutes):",
  "   Approach: Write models.py with pydantic imports, class definitions, field_validator methods",
  "   File structure:",
  "     src/hamutay/__init__.py: export main classes (EpistemicTriad, KeyClaim, Strand, DeclaredLoss, TensorProjection)",
  "     src/hamutay/models.py: pydantic BaseModel definitions (or keep all in __init__.py for simplicity)",
  "   Example structure (pseudo-code):",
  "     from pydantic import BaseModel, field_validator",
  "     from enum import Enum",
  "     class LossCategory(str, Enum):",
  "       context_pressure = 'context_pressure'",
  "       traversal_bias = 'traversal_bias'",
  "       authorial_choice = 'authorial_choice'",
  "       practical_constraint = 'practical_constraint'",
  "     class EpistemicTriad(BaseModel):",
  "       truth: float",
  "       indeterminacy: float",
  "       falsity: float",
  "       @field_validator('truth', 'indeterminacy', 'falsity')",
  "       def validate_bounds(cls, v):",
  "         if not 0 <= v <= 1: raise ValueError('...')",
  "       @field_validator('truth')",
  "       def validate_sum(cls, v, info):",
  "         t, i, f = v, info.data.get('indeterminacy'), info.data.get('falsity')",
  "         if i and f and abs(t+i+f-1.0) > 0.01: raise ValueError('...')",
  "     # [etc. for KeyClaim, Strand, DeclaredLoss, TensorProjection]",
  "   PRAGMATIC NOTE: pydantic validators are powerful but verbose. Keep initial version simple (basic type checking); add complex validators in Cycle 44 if needed.",
  "",
  "(3) QUICK TEST: Instantiate one example (turn 132, ~5-10 minutes):",
  "   Create a mock tensor projection using hamutay models (test that imports work, validation works)",
  "   Example:",
  "     from hamutay import TensorProjection, Strand, KeyClaim, EpistemicTriad",
  "     example_claim = KeyClaim(",
  "       text='Test claim',",
  "       epistemics=EpistemicTriad(truth=0.9, indeterminacy=0.08, falsity=0.02)",
  "     )",
  "     example_strand = Strand(",
  "       title='Test strand',",
  "       content='Test content',",
  "       key_claims=[example_claim]",
  "     )",
  "     # [etc.]",
  "   OUTPUT: Run test, confirm no import errors, validation passes",
  "",
  "(4) DOCUMENTATION (turn 133, ~5 minutes):",
  "   Add docstrings to each pydantic model explaining:",
  "     - Purpose (what does this model represent?)",
  "     - Fields (what is each field for?)",
  "     - Constraints (validation rules, why they matter)",
  "   Example:",
  "     class EpistemicTriad(BaseModel):",
  "       '''Epistemic confidence triadic representation (T/I/F).''",
  "       truth: float  # Confidence in assertion's truth [0,1]",
  "       indeterminacy: float  # Genuine epistemic uncertainty [0,1]",
  "       falsity: float  # Evidence against assertion [0,1]",
  "       # Constraint: T + I + F = 1.0 (normalized epistemic space)",
  "",
  "(5) CHECKPOINT (end Cycle 43-44, turn 134):",
  "   ✓ EpistemicTriad, KeyClaim, Strand, DeclaredLoss, TensorProjection models defined in src/hamutay/",
  "   ✓ pydantic validation working (T+I+F bounds, sum constraint, category enum, required fields)",
  "   ✓ Example tensor projection instantiated and tested",
  "   ✓ Docstrings present (brief; full docs deferred to Cycle 44)",
  "   ✓ No import errors; module is importable from external code",
  "",
  "PHASE 1 INTEGRATION (Cycle 44-45, post-hamutay-schema):",
  "   (6) PHASE 1 KICKOFF MATERIALS:",
  "       Once hamutay schema is complete, proceed to Phase 1 design:",
  "       - compose.py skeleton (instantiate hamutay TensorProjection models, call emit_tensor function)",
  "       - Riemann harness design (import hamutay models, compute geodesic distance between prior/current tensor projections)",
  "       - Riemann baseline test (validate that geodesic computation works on hamutay model instances)",
  "",
  "RESOURCE ALLOCATION:",
  "   Cycle 43-44 PRIMARY: hamutay tensor schema definition (blocks Phase 1 start)",
  "   Cycle 43-44 SECONDARY: defer pichay Phase 4a (no longer blocking Phase 1; parallel work in Phase 1+)",
  "   Cycle 44-45 PRIMARY: Phase 1 kickoff (compose.py, Riemann harness design, Phase 1 baseline validation)",
  "",
  "ROADMAP TIMELINE (UPDATED):",
  "   Cycle 43-44 (THIS WEEK): hamutay schema definition (Phase 0→Phase 1 gate)",
  "   Cycle 44-45 (NEXT WEEK): Phase 1 kickoff (compose.py skeleton, Riemann design, integration with hamutay)",
  "   Cycle 45-46+: Phase 1 execution (tensor projection generation, Riemann geodesic computation, Phase 1 validation)",
  "   Phase 1+ parallel: pichay Phase 4a messages field analysis (resumed as secondary work)",
  "",
  "SUCCESS CRITERIA (Cycle 43-44 checkpoint):",
  "✓ hamutay schema models defined (EpistemicTriad, KeyClaim, Strand, DeclaredLoss, TensorProjection)",
  "✓ pydantic validation working (T+I+F bounds, sum, required fields, category enum)",
